  • Unbounce: "What is a landing page?"
  • Andrew Chen: "The highest ROI way to increase signups"
  • The Startup Pyramid - Sean Ellis

  • Customer Development & Validation

    • "the four steps to epiphany"
    • "the entrepreneur's guide to customer development"
    • "the ultimate list of customer development questions"

  • Email marketing

    • mailchimp.com/resources
    • mailcharts
    • Kalzumeius
    • Subject line cheat sheet for startups (Kalzumeus)
    • Mailchimp "Best practices"

Having a landing page is ESSENTIAL

  • You can make a landing page for a product that doesn't exist yet

  • It's all about the high conversion rate

    • Two types:
    • Lead generation (compiling data like emails)
    • Click-through (subscriptions)

Customer Development & Validation ("How to talk to people")

  • The Law of Contraction

    • You can compete when you narrow your focus
    • "A brand becomes stronger when you narrow its focus"

  • Is your product something people actually need?

    • Are you a vitamin or a painkiller?

  • Come up with a hypothesis about:

    1. who your target audience is
    2. what problem it solves for them

  • 3 Questions to Ask

    1. have you ever had a problem with X?
    2. tell me about a time
    3. What would be your ideal solution?
    4. Will you pay $X for this product?

Copywriting

  • Copy is what you write to get people to do something

  • 3 important parts

    • Headline
    • Be specific and succint

    • Quickly reflect the expectations of the visitor

      • Formulas
      • Get [desired result] in [desirable time period]
      • X (it's about [...])
      • [undesired result] + [mysterious solution]
    • Body copy
    • Focus on benefits for user ("What's in it for me?")
    • Call-to-action
    • Lower the barrier
    • Make it clear where you want someone to click
    • Tell them exactly what you want them to do

Email Marketing

  • 1 way most people communicate

  • 3 kinds of email

    • Drip/lifecycle -> campaign series of emails to build up interest
    • Newsletter/broadcast
    • Transactional -> triggered by on-site or in-app activity

  • Drip structure (10 or 28 days)

    • Day 0: incentive
    • Days 1 and 4: education
    • Day 8: announcement
    • Day 9: hard sell
    • Day 10: reminder

  • Email copy

    • Put 80% of your effort into the subject line
    • Body copy
    • Call to action
    • Qualify
    • "If X, Y, Z, you shouldn't buy"
    • "If ABC, you are a good fit"
    • Add a P.S. section
    • Confirmation emails should have the incentive someone gets for signing up
